# iOS-Specific Libraries & References
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Covers open-source iOS communication, backup parsing, and
# jailbreak-ecosystem tools the project relies on or references.

# ── LIBIMOBILEDEVICE ORGANIZATION — PROJECT DECISIONS ────────
# Source org: https://github.com/libimobiledevice
# Windows binaries: obtain pre-built community releases rather than
# compiling from source. Drop EXEs and DLLs into /bin.

#  [YES] libimobiledevice
#    Source:  https://github.com/libimobiledevice/libimobiledevice
#    Purpose: Core C library for iOS USB communication.
#             Fallback layer when pymobiledevice3 hits version-specific gaps.
#    EXEs to bundle in /bin:
#      idevicebackup2.exe    — backup/restore fallback
#      ideviceinfo.exe       — device info query
#      idevicepair.exe       — pairing and trust management
#    DLLs to bundle in /bin:
#      libimobiledevice-1.0.dll
#      libssl-3-x64.dll
#      libcrypto-3-x64.dll
#      libgcc_s_seh-1.dll
#      libwinpthread-1.dll

#  [YES] libplist
#    Source:  https://github.com/libimobiledevice/libplist
#    Purpose: Apple .plist binary/XML parsing. Required dependency of
#             libimobiledevice. Also used standalone for plist file work.
#    DLLs to bundle in /bin:
#      libplist-2.0.dll
#      libplist++-2.0.dll

#  [YES] libusbmuxd
#    Source:  https://github.com/libimobiledevice/libusbmuxd
#    Purpose: USB multiplexing layer. Required dependency of libimobiledevice.
#             Handles the low-level USB socket that all iOS communication flows through.
#    DLLs to bundle in /bin:
#      libusbmuxd-2.0.dll

#  [YES] libimobiledevice-glue
#    Source:  https://github.com/libimobiledevice/libimobiledevice-glue
#    Purpose: Shared utility functions (threads, sockets, logging) required by
#             libimobiledevice. Will not build or run without it.
#    DLLs to bundle in /bin:
#      libimobiledevice-glue-1.0.dll

#  [YES] ideviceinstaller
#    Source:  https://github.com/libimobiledevice/ideviceinstaller
#    Purpose: Install, list, and uninstall apps on iOS via CLI.
#             Used by inject_applist_ios for app installation on destination device.
#    EXEs to bundle in /bin:
#      ideviceinstaller.exe

#  [MAYBE] libirecovery
#    Source:  https://github.com/libimobiledevice/libirecovery
#    Purpose: DFU and recovery mode communication.
#             Not needed for standard or jailbreak transfers.
#             Reserve for future edge case: device stuck in recovery during transfer.
#    Decision: Downloaded and held. Do not bundle in /bin unless recovery
#              mode handling is explicitly added as a feature.
#    DLLs (if activated):
#      libirecovery-1.0.dll

#  [NO] usbmuxd
#    Source:  https://github.com/libimobiledevice/usbmuxd
#    Reason:  This IS the daemon. On Windows, Apple provides this via
#             AppleMobileDeviceService.exe (installed with iTunes/Apple Devices).
#             Installing the OSS version would conflict with Apple's service.

#  [NO] ifuse
#    Source:  https://github.com/libimobiledevice/ifuse
#    Reason:  FUSE filesystem mount — Linux/macOS only. No Windows support.

#  [NO] idevicerestore
#    Source:  https://github.com/libimobiledevice/idevicerestore
#    Reason:  Full firmware/IPSW restore tool. Completely outside project scope.

#  [NO] libtatsu
#    Source:  https://github.com/libimobiledevice/libtatsu
#    Reason:  Apple TSS server communication for firmware signing. Out of scope.

#  [NO] ideviceactivation
#    Source:  https://github.com/libimobiledevice/ideviceactivation
#    Reason:  Device activation protocol. Out of scope.

#  [NO] libgpod
#    Source:  https://github.com/libimobiledevice/libgpod
#    Reason:  Legacy iPod media library. Irrelevant to this project.

# ── PYTHON PACKAGES — iOS LAYER DECISIONS ────────────────────

#  [YES] pymobiledevice3
#    Source:  https://github.com/doronz88/pymobiledevice3
#    Install: pip install pymobiledevice3
#    Purpose: Primary iOS communication layer. Pure Python. PyInstaller-bundleable.
#             Covers the full Apple USB protocol stack without needing C libs.
#    Services used:
#      com.apple.mobilebackup2      — backup and restore
#      com.apple.afc                — Media partition file access (stock)
#      com.apple.afc2               — Full root filesystem (jailbreak)
#      com.apple.mobile.lockdown    — Pairing, device info
#      com.apple.mobile.installation_proxy — App install/list
#      com.apple.healthd            — Health data (jailbreak)
#      com.apple.syslog_relay       — Device logs (debug use)

#  [YES] iOSbackup
#    Source:  https://github.com/avibrazil/iOSbackup
#    Install: pip install iOSbackup
#    Purpose: Parses Apple MobileSync backup format.
#             Reads Manifest.db, resolves domain + relative path to backup file hash,
#             extracts specific files from the backup folder by SHA1 hash lookup.
#             Used by backup_parser_ios.

#  [NO] pyirecovery
#    Source:  https://github.com/exploit3dguy/pyirecovery
#    Install: pip install pyirecovery
#    Reason:  Python wrapper for DFU/recovery mode. Mirrors libirecovery in Python.
#             Not needed unless recovery mode edge cases are added later.
#             Excluded to keep the dependency footprint lean.
#             Revisit if recovery mode support is ever scoped in.

# ── JAILBREAK ECOSYSTEM REFERENCES ───────────────────────────
# These live on the phone, not the PC. They are not downloaded or bundled.

AFC2 (Apple File Conduit 2)
  Source:    Cydia/Sileo package — installed on jailbroken device by user
  Purpose:   Exposes full root filesystem over USB via the com.apple.afc2 service.
  Detection: PC attempts to open com.apple.afc2 via pymobiledevice3 —
             success confirms jailbroken + AFC2 installed.

ldrestart (userspace reboot)
  Source:    Available on jailbroken device via Substrate/launchctl
  Command:   ldrestart
  Purpose:   Restarts all user-space processes without kernel reboot.
             Called by daemon_restarter_ios after data injection.
             Safe on all jailbreak types. Does not break tethered jailbreaks.

Substitute / Cydia Substrate / Ellekit
  Purpose:   Jailbreak tweak injection frameworks.
             Not used directly — their presence on the device confirms
             an active jailbreak environment during privilege_detector probing.

keychain-dumper  (reference only)
  Source:    https://github.com/ptoomey3/keychain-dumper
  Purpose:   Reference implementation for iOS keychain extraction on jailbreak.
             The project reimplements this via pymobiledevice3 + direct SQLite
             access to /private/var/Keychains/keychain-2.db

Filza File Manager  (reference only)
  Purpose:   Reference for full filesystem navigation on jailbroken devices.
             Not used directly — informs AFC2 path conventions used in extractors.

# ── DATABASE SCHEMAS (documented, not bundled) ───────────────
sms.db
  Tables: message, handle, chat, chat_message_join, attachment, message_attachment_join
  Reference: https://github.com/ReagentX/imessage-exporter

AddressBook.sqlitedb
  Tables: ABPerson, ABMultiValue, ABMultiValueLabel, ABMultiValueEntry

CallHistory.storedata  (Core Data)
  Tables: ZCALLRECORD (Z_PK, ZDURATION, ZDATE, ZADDRESS, ZORIGINATED, ZANSWERED)

healthdb.sqlite + healthdb_secure.sqlite
  Tables: samples, quantity_samples, category_samples, workouts, workout_activities
  Reference: https://github.com/nicholasstephan/HealthSQL

NoteStore.sqlite
  Tables: ZICCLOUDSYNCINGOBJECT (ZTITLE, ZBODY, ZCREATIONDATE, ZMODIFICATIONDATE)
  Body format: Gzip-compressed protobuf (Apple proprietary)
  Note: Plain text recoverable. Rich formatting (tables, drawings) partial or lost.

Calendar.sqlitedb
  Tables: ZCALENDARITEM, ZRECURRENCERULE, ZATTACHMENT, ZPARTICIPANT
